title: corrigiola telephiifolia subsp. imbricata
intro: Corrigiola telephiifolia subsp. imbricata, also known as creeping juvinka or creeping virguncula, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Madderaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Corrigiola telephiifolia subsp. imbricata include its ability to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Corrigiola telephiifolia subsp. imbricata can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to support wound healing and skin health.
